title: Opening the Stacks
desc: Castle Pillar's public routes have to be assembled every morning before anyone can read in peace.

steps.text:
Castle Pillar does not open by unlocking a door. It opens by rearranging itself into something visitors can survive. At dawn the interior is all half-turns and unfinished alignments: stairs ending at blank walls, landings tilted into shafts of light, galleries waiting for the right gear sequence to become hallways again.

A gear tender and an archivist stand in the entry chamber with the calm of creatures who do this every day and still do not take it lightly. Brass waymarkers wait in one crate. Counterweight keys hang from the tender's belt in ascending size. Somewhere higher in the pillar, a bell rings once, and several floors above you answer by shifting with a long, patient grind.

"Public route first," the archivist says. "If that is right, the rest of the day behaves." You can take the upper stair with the waymarkers, or head into the gear hall and release the first counterweight locks that let the library settle into its morning shape.

options.label: Carry the brass waymarkers up the turning stair
Release the counterweight locks in the gear hall

steps.text:
The upper stair moves slowly enough to trust and just quickly enough to remind you who is in charge. One landing flattens as you step onto it. Another rotates a quarter turn while a shaft of pale light slides across the wall like a measured hand. You place each brass marker where the archivist indicates: public reading rooms this way, catalog court ahead, do not trust the next door until the second bell.

As the markers go in, the route begins to feel real. What had been a puzzle becomes a path. By the time you reach the third landing, the stairs behind you have aligned into a clear descending line for the first time all morning. Far below, the archivist looks up and gives an approving nod.

For a moment you stop beside a narrow window slit. Outside is only the dim vastness around the pillar. Inside is a whole library becoming legible by degrees. Castle Pillar is still a machine of secrets, but under the right hands it also becomes a place where someone might simply come to read.

options.label: Finish the route and watch the doors align

steps.text:
The gear hall sits below the public floors, all stone teeth, hanging chains, and shafts running upward into darkness. Nothing here looks decorative, but the sheer scale of it creates its own kind of grandeur. Every movement in Castle Pillar begins in rooms like this, where heavy things are persuaded to move with precision instead of violence.

The tender hands you a key and points out the lock sequence scratched into a brass plate. You work the releases one at a time. First the near weight drops with a controlled slide. Then a second catch opens and a line of gears overhead begins to turn. Somewhere beyond the wall, a whole staircase must be flattening into place. The hall fills with the deep measured language of counterweights doing honest work.

When the final lock releases, the tender rests one hand on the rail and listens. "Good morning," he says to the machinery, and the machinery, in its fashion, answers. The pillar is awake now. Not all of it. Not the parts anyone sensible mentions. But enough of it for another day of knowledge to remain accessible.

options.label: Hang the key back on its peg
